name: Romagna Albana (Albana)
desc: Notes of almond, peach, and honey with a distinct tannic grip for a white.

name: Colli Bolognesi Pignoletto (Pignoletto)
desc: Light, crisp, and slightly floral sparkling or still white.

name: Ramandolo (Verduzzo Friulano)
desc: Sweet, slightly tannic dessert wine featuring dried apricot.

name: Colli Orientali del Friuli Picolit (Picolit)
desc: Precious, sweet dessert wine showing delicate floral and honey nuances.

name: Lison (Tai / Friulano)
desc: Refreshing, slightly almond-scented white wine.

name: Rosazzo (Friulano, Sauvignon Blanc)
desc: Elegant and complex white blend with mineral and herbaceous hints.

name: Franciacorta (Chardonnay, Pinot Nero)
desc: Italy's premium traditional method sparkler. Brioche, citrus, and fine bubbles.

name: Valtellina Superiore (Nebbiolo)
desc: Alpine Nebbiolo showing lighter body but high tannins and acidity with cherry and rose notes.

name: Sforzato di Valtellina (Nebbiolo)
desc: Made from dried Nebbiolo grapes (appassimento), rich, robust, and alcoholic.

name: Oltrepò Pavese Metodo Classico (Pinot Nero)
desc: Structured, elegant traditional method sparkling wine often with red berry notes.

name: Moscato di Scanzo (Moscato di Scanzo)
desc: Rare, sweet red wine featuring rose, sage, and dark fruit aromas.

name: Alta Langa (Pinot Nero, Chardonnay)
desc: Complex traditional method sparkling wine from Piedmont with aging potential.

name: Asti / Moscato d'Asti (Moscato Bianco)
desc: Low-alcohol, intensely aromatic and sweet sparkling wine.

name: Barbaresco (Nebbiolo)
desc: Elegant Nebbiolo with notes of rose, tar, and cherry; slightly softer than Barolo.

name: Barbera d'Asti (Barbera)
desc: Juicy, high-acid red with dark cherry and blackberry flavors.

name: Barbera del Monferrato Superiore
desc: Richer expression of Barbera with ripe fruit and sometimes slight oak aging.

name: Barolo (Nebbiolo)
desc: The 'King of Wines'. Powerful structured tannins, high acid, complex tar and rose aromas.

name: Brachetto d'Acqui (Brachetto)
desc: Sweet, lightly sparkling red with intense strawberry and rose petal notes.

name: Dogliani (Dolcetto)
desc: Rich, tannic presentation of Dolcetto with dark fruit and almond hints.

name: Dolcetto di Diano d'Alba
desc: Fruit-forward red wine with soft acidity and noticeable tannins.

name: Dolcetto di Ovada Superiore
desc: Intense Dolcetto expression requiring aging to soften its tannins.

name: Erbaluce di Caluso (Erbaluce)
desc: Bright, high-acid white wine featuring apple and alpine herbs.

name: Gattinara (Nebbiolo)
desc: Earthy and mineral-focused Nebbiolo grown on volcanic soils.

name: Gavi (Cortese)
desc: Bone-dry, crisp white with lemon and almond blossom notes.

name: Ghemme (Nebbiolo)
desc: Northern Piedmont Nebbiolo that is highly structured and long-lived.

name: Canelli (Moscato Bianco)
desc: Expressive Moscato d'Asti sub-zone known for intense aromatics.

name: Nizza (Barbera)
desc: Top-tier Barbera displaying dark cherry, plum, and integrated oak.

name: Roero (Nebbiolo / Arneis)
desc: Approachable Nebbiolo with red fruits and sandy soil minerality.

name: Ruchè di Castagnole Monferrato
desc: Aromatic red wine with bold rose, pepper, and wild berry scents.

name: Terre Alfieri (Arneis/Nebbiolo)
desc: Floral and slightly nutty Arneis (or lighter style Nebbiolo).

name: Amarone della Valpolicella
desc: Rich, powerful red made from partially dried grapes; flavors of fig and chocolate.

name: Asolo Prosecco (Glera)
desc: Crisp, fruity sparkling wine with notes of pear and green apple.

name: Bagnoli Friularo (Raboso Piave)
desc: Highly astringent, high-acid red wine often requiring extensive aging.

name: Bardolino Superiore
desc: Light to medium-bodied red focusing on bright cherry fruit and subtle spice.

name: Colli di Conegliano (Manzoni Bianco / Cabernet)
desc: A diverse appellation offering structured reds and floral whites.

name: Colli Euganei Fior d'Arancio
desc: Intense orange blossom and sweet citrus aroma.

name: Conegliano Valdobbiadene Prosecco
desc: Premium Prosecco showing elegant floral, peach, and crisp apple nuances.

name: Montello Rosso (Bordeaux Blend)
desc: Robust structured red blend made primarily of Cabernet and Merlot.

name: Piave Malanotte (Raboso Piave)
desc: Powerful, dark, and highly structured red with prominent tannin.

name: Recioto della Valpolicella
desc: Rich, sweet dessert wine featuring chocolate, cherry, and spice.

name: Recioto di Gambellara (Garganega)
desc: Golden sweet wine with honeyed, almond, and dried apricot notes.

name: Recioto di Soave (Garganega)
desc: A luscious, complex sweet wine exhibiting rich floral and dried fruit character.

name: Soave Superiore (Garganega)
desc: Elevated Soave offering almond, melon, and chamomile flavors.

name: Brunello di Montalcino (Sangiovese)
desc: Rich, complex Sangiovese known for its exceptional aging potential.

name: Carmignano (Sangiovese/Cabernet)
desc: Historic blend of Sangiovese and Cabernet Sauvignon, bringing structure and dark fruit.

name: Chianti (Sangiovese)
desc: Bright red cherry, earth, and savory herbs with high food-friendly acidity.

name: Chianti Classico (Sangiovese)
desc: The historic heart of Chianti. Structured, tart cherry, and dusty tannin.

name: Elba Aleatico Passito (Aleatico)
desc: Rare, sweet, aromatic red with distinctive rose and red berry nuances.

name: Montecucco Sangiovese
desc: A robust Sangiovese expression resembling wines from neighboring Montalcino.

name: Morellino di Scansano (Sangiovese)
desc: A fleshier, softer style of Sangiovese grown near the coast.

name: Rosso della Val di Cornia
desc: A blend of local Sangiovese with international varieties like Cabernet.

name: Suvereto (Bordeaux Blend / Sangiovese)
desc: Intense and rich 'Super Tuscan' style showcasing dark fruit and structure.

name: Vernaccia di San Gimignano
desc: Classic Tuscan white with crisp acidity, yellow apple, and a slightly bitter almond finish.

name: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano
desc: Plum, dark cherry, and spice with fine, integrated tannins.

name: Castelli di Jesi Verdicchio Riserva
desc: Aged Verdicchio showing incredible texture, almond, and complex minerality.

name: Conero (Montepulciano)
desc: Dark fruit, black cherry, and chocolate notes from the Montepulciano grape.

name: Offida (Pecorino/Passerina)
desc: Floral and herbal whites, or robust reds built on the Montepulciano grape.

name: Verdicchio di Matelica Riserva
desc: More sharply acidic and mineral-driven due to the cooler inland terroir.

name: Vernaccia di Serrapetrona
desc: Fascinating sparkling red, often slightly sweet, made with a triple-fermentation method.

name: Montefalco Sagrantino
desc: One of the world's most tannic wines, featuring dark blackberry and smoke.

name: Torgiano Rosso Riserva (Sangiovese)
desc: Umbria’s elegant answer to Chianti Classico, built for aging.

name: Montepulciano d'Abruzzo Colline Teramane
desc: Rich dark fruit, cocoa, and an earthy, robust character.

name: Cannellino di Frascati
desc: Historical sweet wine from Rome featuring honey and white peach.

name: Cesanese del Piglio
desc: Red berry and distinct pepper/spice notes with a bright character.

name: Frascati Superiore
desc: Crisp, zesty, and slightly almond-tinged white from volcanic soils.

name: Aglianico del Taburno
desc: Grippy and acidic Aglianico presenting dark plum, earth, and volcanic minerality.

name: Fiano di Avellino
desc: Fuller-bodied white featuring hazelnut, pear, and honey.

name: Greco di Tufo
desc: Intensely mineral white with slight tannic structure and lemony brightness.

name: Taurasi (Aglianico)
desc: The 'Barolo of the South', capable of decades of aging; tar and smoke.

name: Aglianico del Vulture Superiore
desc: High-altitude volcanic Aglianico, showcasing refined power and distinct pepper notes.

name: Castel del Monte Bombino Nero
desc: Italy's only DOCG dedicated primarily to Rosé (Rosato). Bright and vibrant.

name: Castel del Monte Nero di Troia Riserva
desc: Dark, brooding red with violet aromas and firm tannic grip.

name: Castel del Monte Rosso Riserva
desc: Usually a blend, featuring dark plum and licorice flavors.

name: Primitivo di Manduria Dolce Naturale
desc: Rich, jammy dessert wine showing massive fruit and chocolate richness.

name: Vermentino di Gallura
desc: Saline, herbaceous, and citrusy white wine; perfectly matches seafood.

name: Cerasuolo di Vittoria
desc: Blend of Nero d'Avola and Frappato, bringing together dark fruit and lively strawberry.

name: Bolgheri (Cabernet Blend)
desc: A powerhouse Super Tuscan with lush dark fruit and integrated oak.

name: Rosso di Montalcino (Sangiovese)
desc: The accessible 'younger sibling' to Brunello, rich with red cherry.

name: Rosso di Montepulciano (Sangiovese)
desc: Approachable and fruity, a daily-drinking Tuscan classic.

name: Langhe Nebbiolo
desc: A slightly softer introduction to Nebbiolo from the Langhe hills.

name: Barbera d'Alba
desc: Rich, fleshy, and highly acidic; distinct blackberry and vanilla character.

name: Dolcetto d'Alba
desc: Deeply colored everyday red focusing on dark plum and bitter almond.

name: Nebbiolo d'Alba
desc: Classic Nebbiolo revealing floral, cherry, and spice over a firm structure.

name: Valpolicella (Corvina Blend)
desc: Bright and lively red showing sour cherry and a slightly tangy finish.

name: Valpolicella Ripasso
desc: 'Baby Amarone'; darker fruit profile, velvet texture, and slightly fuller body.

name: Lugana (Turbiana)
desc: Peach and almond notes with an appealing rounded texture.

name: Bardolino
desc: Crunchy red fruit and light body; excellent slightly chilled.

name: Soave (Garganega)
desc: Fresh daily white offering melon, sweet almond, and lively acidity.

name: Orvieto (Grechetto/Trebbiano)
desc: Classic central Italian white, often crisp and slightly nutty.

name: Est! Est!! Est!!! di Montefiascone
desc: Light, crisp, and historically famous quaffing white from Lazio.

name: Lacryma Christi del Vesuvio
desc: Volcanic red displaying smoky, bright plum, and cherry characters.

name: Ischia (Biancolella)
desc: Coastal, saline white wine featuring lemon peel and wild herbs.

name: Etna Rosso (Nerello Mascalese)
desc: 'Mediterranean Burgundy'; elegant, smoky, and pale colored red.

name: Etna Bianco (Carricante)
desc: Highly mineral, citrus-driven, and racy white reflecting its volcanic origin.

name: Marsala (Grillo/Catarratto)
desc: Fortified wine with caramelized, nutty, and dried fruit flavors.

name: Salice Salentino (Negroamaro)
desc: Earthy, dark-fruited red exhibiting baked plum and subtle licorice.

name: Primitivo di Manduria
desc: Lush, jammy dark fruit with high alcohol and sweet spice.

name: Locorotondo
desc: A surprisingly crisp, dry southern white wine with subtle aromatics.

name: Montepulciano d'Abruzzo
desc: Incredibly popular pizza-pairing red; dark fruit, mild tannin.

name: Trebbiano d'Abruzzo
desc: Neutral, highly drinkable everyday white; sometimes crafted into ageworthy masterpieces.

name: Cerasuolo d'Abruzzo
desc: A dark, structured rosato possessing surprising tannin and cherry fruit.

name: Verdicchio dei Castelli di Jesi
desc: A classic fish wine bearing green apple, citrus, and a bitter almond end.

name: Rosso Piceno (Montepulciano/Sangiovese)
desc: Value-driven, highly drinkable red blend balancing cherry and earth.

name: Cannonau di Sardegna
desc: Sardinian Grenache showcasing strawberry jam, pepper, and Mediterranean scrub.

name: Monica di Sardegna
desc: Soft, fruity red wine featuring blackberry and a touch of earthiness.

name: Carignano del Sulcis
desc: Sun-drenched, sandy soil red presenting dark plum, spice, and robust structure.

name: Prosecco (Glera)
desc: Highly popular, fruity, and approachable standard sparkling wine.

name: Lambrusco di Sorbara
desc: Frizzante, light pink-red offering sharp acidity and vibrant strawberry fruit.

name: Lambrusco Grasparossa di Castelvetro
desc: Darker, more tannic semi-sparkling red pairing perfectly with rich charcuterie.

name: Frascati (Malvasia/Trebbiano)
desc: Approachable, crisp historical white consumed in Rome's trattorias.
